chapter_context.summary: Pronounces royal judgments on Jehoahaz, Jehoiakim, and Coniah (Jehoiachin), declaring that Coniah will write himself childless and never prosper.

outline.text: Exhortation to Royal House of David to Execute Justice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Exhortation to Royal House of David to Execute Justice (22:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Judgment on Shallum (Jehoahaz): Weeping for Exile
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judgment on Shallum (Jehoahaz): Weeping for Exile (22:10-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Denouncing Jehoiakim's Extravagant Palace of Unrighteousness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Denouncing Jehoiakim's Extravagant Palace of Unrighteousness (22:13-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.
This section focuses on Rejection of Coniah (Jehoiachin): Written Childless (22:20-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 22, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
